timeline_description: Major political, military, and dynastic events in the rise, expansion, fragmentation, and decline of the Mongol Empire.
title: Temujin is proclaimed Genghis Khan
description: At a kurultai on the Mongolian steppe, Temujin is proclaimed Genghis Khan, marking the formal founding of the Mongol Empire.

timeline_description: Major political, military, and dynastic events in the rise, expansion, fragmentation, and decline of the Mongol Empire.
title: Western Xia submits
description: After early campaigns, the Tangut kingdom of Western Xia submits to Mongol power and becomes a tributary state.

timeline_description: Major political, military, and dynastic events in the rise, expansion, fragmentation, and decline of the Mongol Empire.
title: Invasion of the Jin dynasty begins
description: Genghis Khan launches a major invasion of the Jin dynasty, opening the long Mongol conquest of northern China.

timeline_description: Major political, military, and dynastic events in the rise, expansion, fragmentation, and decline of the Mongol Empire.
title: Zhongdu falls to the Mongols
description: Mongol forces capture Zhongdu, the Jin capital near present-day Beijing, after a destructive campaign.

timeline_description: Major political, military, and dynastic events in the rise, expansion, fragmentation, and decline of the Mongol Empire.
title: Kara-Khitai is absorbed
description: The Mongols destroy the Kara-Khitai Khanate, extending their reach deep into Central Asia.

timeline_description: Major political, military, and dynastic events in the rise, expansion, fragmentation, and decline of the Mongol Empire.
title: Khwarazmian campaign begins
description: Genghis Khan invades the Khwarazmian Empire after a diplomatic rupture, beginning one of the Mongols' most devastating campaigns.

timeline_description: Major political, military, and dynastic events in the rise, expansion, fragmentation, and decline of the Mongol Empire.
title: Mongols win at the Kalka River
description: A Mongol expedition defeats a coalition of Rus and Cuman forces at the Battle of the Kalka River.

timeline_description: Major political, military, and dynastic events in the rise, expansion, fragmentation, and decline of the Mongol Empire.
title: Genghis Khan dies
description: Genghis Khan dies during the campaign against Western Xia, leaving an immense but still expanding empire.

timeline_description: Major political, military, and dynastic events in the rise, expansion, fragmentation, and decline of the Mongol Empire.
title: Ogedei becomes Great Khan
description: At a kurultai, Ogedei is elected Great Khan and continues large-scale imperial expansion.

timeline_description: Major political, military, and dynastic events in the rise, expansion, fragmentation, and decline of the Mongol Empire.
title: Jin dynasty is destroyed
description: Mongol forces complete the conquest of the Jin dynasty, securing control over northern China.

timeline_description: Major political, military, and dynastic events in the rise, expansion, fragmentation, and decline of the Mongol Empire.
title: Mongol invasion of the Rus principalities
description: Batu Khan begins the major westward invasion that devastates the Rus principalities.

timeline_description: Major political, military, and dynastic events in the rise, expansion, fragmentation, and decline of the Mongol Empire.
title: Victories at Legnica and Mohi
description: Mongol armies defeat European forces in Poland and Hungary, demonstrating the empire's reach into Central Europe.

timeline_description: Major political, military, and dynastic events in the rise, expansion, fragmentation, and decline of the Mongol Empire.
title: Guyuk is enthroned
description: Guyuk, son of Ogedei, is enthroned as Great Khan after a contested succession.

timeline_description: Major political, military, and dynastic events in the rise, expansion, fragmentation, and decline of the Mongol Empire.
title: Mongke becomes Great Khan
description: Mongke is elected Great Khan, beginning a new phase of centralized imperial campaigning.

timeline_description: Major political, military, and dynastic events in the rise, expansion, fragmentation, and decline of the Mongol Empire.
title: Baghdad is captured
description: Hulegu's army captures Baghdad and destroys the Abbasid caliphate's capital, a turning point in Middle Eastern history.

timeline_description: Major political, military, and dynastic events in the rise, expansion, fragmentation, and decline of the Mongol Empire.
title: Mongol advance is checked at Ayn Jalut
description: Mamluk forces defeat a Mongol army at Ayn Jalut, halting further Mongol expansion into Egypt.

timeline_description: Major political, military, and dynastic events in the rise, expansion, fragmentation, and decline of the Mongol Empire.
title: Kublai Khan claims the throne
description: Kublai Khan is proclaimed Great Khan, though his rule is contested in a civil war with Ariq Boke.

timeline_description: Major political, military, and dynastic events in the rise, expansion, fragmentation, and decline of the Mongol Empire.
title: Yuan dynasty is proclaimed
description: Kublai formally proclaims the Yuan dynasty in China, recasting Mongol rule in imperial Chinese terms.

timeline_description: Major political, military, and dynastic events in the rise, expansion, fragmentation, and decline of the Mongol Empire.
title: Southern Song is conquered
description: The Mongols complete the conquest of Southern Song China, bringing all of China under Yuan rule.

timeline_description: Major political, military, and dynastic events in the rise, expansion, fragmentation, and decline of the Mongol Empire.
title: Yuan rule in China collapses
description: The Ming capture Khanbaliq and drive the Yuan court north, ending Mongol rule over China proper.

timeline_description: Major events in the development, spread, institutional history, and modern global presence of Buddhism.
title: Birth of Siddhartha Gautama
description: Siddhartha Gautama, who would become the Buddha, is traditionally dated to the 6th century BCE in northern India.

timeline_description: Major events in the development, spread, institutional history, and modern global presence of Buddhism.
title: The Buddha attains enlightenment
description: After years of spiritual search, Siddhartha Gautama attains enlightenment at Bodh Gaya and becomes the Buddha.

timeline_description: Major events in the development, spread, institutional history, and modern global presence of Buddhism.
title: First sermon at Sarnath
description: The Buddha delivers his first sermon, setting forth the Four Noble Truths and founding the sangha.

timeline_description: Major events in the development, spread, institutional history, and modern global presence of Buddhism.
title: The Buddha dies
description: The Buddha's parinirvana marks the end of his earthly life and the beginning of the community's effort to preserve his teachings.

timeline_description: Major events in the development, spread, institutional history, and modern global presence of Buddhism.
title: First Buddhist Council convenes
description: According to tradition, monks gather at Rajagriha to recite and preserve the Buddha's teachings after his death.

timeline_description: Major events in the development, spread, institutional history, and modern global presence of Buddhism.
title: Second Buddhist Council meets
description: A council at Vaishali addresses disciplinary disputes that later traditions associate with early schisms.

timeline_description: Major events in the development, spread, institutional history, and modern global presence of Buddhism.
title: Ashoka adopts and promotes Buddhism
description: The Mauryan emperor Ashoka supports Buddhism and helps turn it into a major transregional religion.

timeline_description: Major events in the development, spread, institutional history, and modern global presence of Buddhism.
title: Third Buddhist Council is held
description: Tradition places a major council at Pataliputra under Ashoka, linked to doctrinal clarification and missionary activity.

timeline_description: Major events in the development, spread, institutional history, and modern global presence of Buddhism.
title: Buddhism reaches Sri Lanka
description: Missionary activity associated with Mahinda establishes Buddhism in Sri Lanka, where Theravada later takes deep root.

timeline_description: Major events in the development, spread, institutional history, and modern global presence of Buddhism.
title: Pali Canon is written down
description: In Sri Lanka, the Theravada canon is committed to writing, preserving a foundational textual tradition.

timeline_description: Major events in the development, spread, institutional history, and modern global presence of Buddhism.
title: Mahayana Buddhism expands
description: Mahayana traditions emerge and spread, reshaping Buddhist thought, practice, and scripture.

timeline_description: Major events in the development, spread, institutional history, and modern global presence of Buddhism.
title: Nagarjuna advances Madhyamaka thought
description: The philosopher Nagarjuna becomes a pivotal figure in the development of Mahayana philosophy.

timeline_description: Major events in the development, spread, institutional history, and modern global presence of Buddhism.
title: Faxian begins his pilgrimage
description: The Chinese monk Faxian departs for India in search of Buddhist texts, reflecting strong trans-Asian Buddhist networks.

timeline_description: Major events in the development, spread, institutional history, and modern global presence of Buddhism.
title: Bodhidharma is linked to Chan tradition
description: Tradition associates Bodhidharma with the transmission of a meditative Buddhist lineage in China that later becomes Chan.

timeline_description: Major events in the development, spread, institutional history, and modern global presence of Buddhism.
title: Buddhism is introduced to Japan
description: Buddhism reaches the Japanese court, where it becomes a major religious and cultural force.

timeline_description: Major events in the development, spread, institutional history, and modern global presence of Buddhism.
title: Buddhism becomes established in Tibet
description: Indian and Tibetan teachers help entrench Buddhism in Tibet, where it develops distinctive institutions and traditions.

timeline_description: Major events in the development, spread, institutional history, and modern global presence of Buddhism.
title: Nalanda is destroyed
description: The destruction of Nalanda becomes a symbol of Buddhism's decline in much of India during the late medieval period.

timeline_description: Major events in the development, spread, institutional history, and modern global presence of Buddhism.
title: Theravada consolidates in mainland Southeast Asia
description: Theravada Buddhism becomes dominant across much of mainland Southeast Asia.

timeline_description: Major events in the development, spread, institutional history, and modern global presence of Buddhism.
title: Mahabodhi Society is founded
description: Anagarika Dharmapala and allies found the Mahabodhi Society, an important force in modern Buddhist revival.

timeline_description: Major events in the development, spread, institutional history, and modern global presence of Buddhism.
title: Ambedkar leads a mass conversion to Buddhism
description: B. R. Ambedkar converts to Buddhism with large numbers of followers, making the religion a modern force in social reform in India.

timeline_title: Timeline of the Thirty Years' War
timeline_description: Key political, military, and diplomatic events in the conflict that transformed Central Europe between 1618 and 1648.
title: Peace of Augsburg sets a fragile settlement
description: The Peace of Augsburg recognizes Lutheranism in the Holy Roman Empire but leaves major confessional tensions unresolved.

timeline_title: Timeline of the Thirty Years' War
timeline_description: Key political, military, and diplomatic events in the conflict that transformed Central Europe between 1618 and 1648.
title: Protestant Union is formed
description: Several Protestant princes create the Protestant Union amid rising fears of Catholic pressure.

timeline_title: Timeline of the Thirty Years' War
timeline_description: Key political, military, and diplomatic events in the conflict that transformed Central Europe between 1618 and 1648.
title: Catholic League is formed
description: Catholic princes organize the Catholic League, deepening the empire's political and religious polarization.

timeline_title: Timeline of the Thirty Years' War
timeline_description: Key political, military, and diplomatic events in the conflict that transformed Central Europe between 1618 and 1648.
title: Defenestration of Prague
description: Bohemian nobles throw imperial officials from Prague Castle, triggering the Bohemian Revolt and the wider war.

timeline_title: Timeline of the Thirty Years' War
timeline_description: Key political, military, and diplomatic events in the conflict that transformed Central Europe between 1618 and 1648.
title: Ferdinand II becomes Holy Roman emperor
description: The accession of Ferdinand II hardens the Habsburg response to the Bohemian challenge.

timeline_title: Timeline of the Thirty Years' War
timeline_description: Key political, military, and diplomatic events in the conflict that transformed Central Europe between 1618 and 1648.
title: Battle of White Mountain
description: Imperial and Catholic League forces crush the Bohemian revolt near Prague.

timeline_title: Timeline of the Thirty Years' War
timeline_description: Key political, military, and diplomatic events in the conflict that transformed Central Europe between 1618 and 1648.
title: Bohemian leaders are executed
description: Imperial authorities execute leading rebels in Prague, symbolizing Habsburg reconquest of Bohemia.

timeline_title: Timeline of the Thirty Years' War
timeline_description: Key political, military, and diplomatic events in the conflict that transformed Central Europe between 1618 and 1648.
title: Christian IV enters the war
description: The king of Denmark intervenes on the Protestant side, widening the conflict.

timeline_title: Timeline of the Thirty Years' War
timeline_description: Key political, military, and diplomatic events in the conflict that transformed Central Europe between 1618 and 1648.
title: Battle of Lutter
description: Catholic League forces defeat Christian IV, weakening Danish intervention.

timeline_title: Timeline of the Thirty Years' War
timeline_description: Key political, military, and diplomatic events in the conflict that transformed Central Europe between 1618 and 1648.
title: Edict of Restitution is issued
description: Ferdinand II attempts to restore Catholic ecclesiastical property, alarming Protestant states.

timeline_title: Timeline of the Thirty Years' War
timeline_description: Key political, military, and diplomatic events in the conflict that transformed Central Europe between 1618 and 1648.
title: Treaty of Lubeck ends Danish phase
description: Denmark withdraws from the war after the Treaty of Lubeck.

timeline_title: Timeline of the Thirty Years' War
timeline_description: Key political, military, and diplomatic events in the conflict that transformed Central Europe between 1618 and 1648.
title: Gustavus Adolphus lands in Germany
description: Sweden enters the war decisively under Gustavus Adolphus.

timeline_title: Timeline of the Thirty Years' War
timeline_description: Key political, military, and diplomatic events in the conflict that transformed Central Europe between 1618 and 1648.
title: Magdeburg is sacked
description: Imperial troops destroy Magdeburg, one of the conflict's most notorious atrocities.

timeline_title: Timeline of the Thirty Years' War
timeline_description: Key political, military, and diplomatic events in the conflict that transformed Central Europe between 1618 and 1648.
title: Battle of Breitenfeld
description: Swedish-led forces win a major victory that reverses Protestant fortunes.

timeline_title: Timeline of the Thirty Years' War
timeline_description: Key political, military, and diplomatic events in the conflict that transformed Central Europe between 1618 and 1648.
title: Battle of Lutzen
description: Sweden wins at Lutzen, but Gustavus Adolphus is killed in battle.

timeline_title: Timeline of the Thirty Years' War
timeline_description: Key political, military, and diplomatic events in the conflict that transformed Central Europe between 1618 and 1648.
title: Battle of Nordlingen
description: Imperial and Spanish forces defeat the Swedes, weakening the Protestant alliance.

timeline_title: Timeline of the Thirty Years' War
timeline_description: Key political, military, and diplomatic events in the conflict that transformed Central Europe between 1618 and 1648.
title: Peace of Prague is concluded
description: Several German Protestant princes reconcile with the emperor, reshaping the conflict.

timeline_title: Timeline of the Thirty Years' War
timeline_description: Key political, military, and diplomatic events in the conflict that transformed Central Europe between 1618 and 1648.
title: France enters the war openly
description: Cardinal Richelieu commits France directly against the Habsburgs, turning the conflict more fully dynastic and geopolitical.

timeline_title: Timeline of the Thirty Years' War
timeline_description: Key political, military, and diplomatic events in the conflict that transformed Central Europe between 1618 and 1648.
title: Battle of Rocroi
description: French forces defeat Spain at Rocroi, signaling a major shift in European military power.

timeline_title: Timeline of the Thirty Years' War
timeline_description: Key political, military, and diplomatic events in the conflict that transformed Central Europe between 1618 and 1648.
title: Peace of Westphalia ends the war
description: The treaties of Westphalia conclude the Thirty Years' War and reshape the political order of Europe.

timeline_description: Major institutional, strategic, and membership milestones in the history of the North Atlantic Treaty Organization.
title: Brussels Treaty is signed
description: Western European states sign the Brussels Treaty, an important precursor to the Atlantic alliance.

timeline_description: Major institutional, strategic, and membership milestones in the history of the North Atlantic Treaty Organization.
title: North Atlantic Treaty is signed
description: Twelve countries sign the North Atlantic Treaty in Washington, creating NATO.

timeline_description: Major institutional, strategic, and membership milestones in the history of the North Atlantic Treaty Organization.
title: Korean War accelerates NATO rearmament
description: The outbreak of war in Korea spurs a faster military build-up within NATO.

timeline_description: Major institutional, strategic, and membership milestones in the history of the North Atlantic Treaty Organization.
title: Eisenhower becomes first SACEUR
description: Dwight D. Eisenhower becomes NATO's first Supreme Allied Commander Europe, helping organize integrated defense.

timeline_description: Major institutional, strategic, and membership milestones in the history of the North Atlantic Treaty Organization.
title: Greece and Turkey join NATO
description: The alliance expands to southeastern Europe with the admission of Greece and Turkey.

timeline_description: Major institutional, strategic, and membership milestones in the history of the North Atlantic Treaty Organization.
title: West Germany joins NATO
description: The Federal Republic of Germany becomes a NATO member, a major Cold War turning point.

timeline_description: Major institutional, strategic, and membership milestones in the history of the North Atlantic Treaty Organization.
title: Harmel Report redefines alliance strategy
description: NATO adopts the Harmel approach, pairing defense with détente and political consultation.

timeline_description: Major institutional, strategic, and membership milestones in the history of the North Atlantic Treaty Organization.
title: Spain joins NATO
description: Spain becomes a member, extending the alliance further into southwestern Europe.

timeline_description: Major institutional, strategic, and membership milestones in the history of the North Atlantic Treaty Organization.
title: Cold War order begins to collapse
description: The fall of the Berlin Wall forces NATO to rethink its mission after decades of confrontation with the Soviet bloc.

timeline_description: Major institutional, strategic, and membership milestones in the history of the North Atlantic Treaty Organization.
title: NATO adopts a new Strategic Concept
description: After the Cold War, NATO reframes its mission around crisis management, cooperation, and collective defense.

timeline_description: Major institutional, strategic, and membership milestones in the history of the North Atlantic Treaty Organization.
title: Partnership for Peace is launched
description: NATO creates the Partnership for Peace to deepen cooperation with non-member states in Europe and Eurasia.

timeline_description: Major institutional, strategic, and membership milestones in the history of the North Atlantic Treaty Organization.
title: NATO deploys IFOR in Bosnia
description: The alliance carries out its first major military operation by helping implement the Dayton peace settlement in Bosnia.

timeline_description: Major institutional, strategic, and membership milestones in the history of the North Atlantic Treaty Organization.
title: Czech Republic, Hungary, and Poland join
description: NATO's first post-Cold War enlargement brings in three former Warsaw Pact states.

timeline_description: Major institutional, strategic, and membership milestones in the history of the North Atlantic Treaty Organization.
title: NATO conducts the Kosovo air campaign
description: Operation Allied Force marks a major alliance intervention in the Balkans.

timeline_description: Major institutional, strategic, and membership milestones in the history of the North Atlantic Treaty Organization.
title: Article 5 is invoked after 9/11
description: For the first time, NATO invokes Article 5 in response to the attacks on the United States.

timeline_description: Major institutional, strategic, and membership milestones in the history of the North Atlantic Treaty Organization.
title: Seven states join in a major enlargement
description: Bulgaria, Estonia, Latvia, Lithuania, Romania, Slovakia, and Slovenia enter NATO.

timeline_description: Major institutional, strategic, and membership milestones in the history of the North Atlantic Treaty Organization.
title: Lisbon Strategic Concept is adopted
description: NATO updates its strategy to address collective defense, crisis management, and cooperative security.

timeline_description: Major institutional, strategic, and membership milestones in the history of the North Atlantic Treaty Organization.
title: Montenegro becomes the 29th ally
description: Montenegro formally joins NATO after depositing its instrument of accession.

timeline_description: Major institutional, strategic, and membership milestones in the history of the North Atlantic Treaty Organization.
title: North Macedonia becomes the 30th ally
description: North Macedonia formally joins NATO, completing a long accession process.

timeline_description: Major institutional, strategic, and membership milestones in the history of the North Atlantic Treaty Organization.
title: Sweden becomes the 32nd ally
description: Sweden joins NATO, following Finland's accession in 2023 and a major shift in Nordic security policy.

timeline_description: Major theoretical and experimental milestones in the development of quantum mechanics and quantum theory.
title: Planck proposes energy quanta
description: Max Planck explains blackbody radiation by treating energy as quantized, launching quantum theory.

timeline_description: Major theoretical and experimental milestones in the development of quantum mechanics and quantum theory.
title: Einstein explains the photoelectric effect
description: Albert Einstein argues that light behaves as quanta, later called photons, extending the quantum idea beyond radiation theory.

timeline_description: Major theoretical and experimental milestones in the development of quantum mechanics and quantum theory.
title: Rutherford proposes the nuclear atom
description: Ernest Rutherford's atomic model creates a crisis classical physics cannot solve, setting the stage for quantum atomic theory.

timeline_description: Major theoretical and experimental milestones in the development of quantum mechanics and quantum theory.
title: Bohr introduces the quantum atom
description: Niels Bohr proposes quantized electron orbits to explain hydrogen spectra.

timeline_description: Major theoretical and experimental milestones in the development of quantum mechanics and quantum theory.
title: Compton effect confirms quantum light
description: Arthur Compton shows that X-rays scatter as particles with momentum, strengthening the photon concept.

timeline_description: Major theoretical and experimental milestones in the development of quantum mechanics and quantum theory.
title: de Broglie proposes matter waves
description: Louis de Broglie suggests that matter has wave-like properties, a central step toward modern quantum mechanics.

timeline_description: Major theoretical and experimental milestones in the development of quantum mechanics and quantum theory.
title: Pauli formulates the exclusion principle
description: Wolfgang Pauli explains key atomic regularities with the exclusion principle.

timeline_description: Major theoretical and experimental milestones in the development of quantum mechanics and quantum theory.
title: Heisenberg creates matrix mechanics
description: Werner Heisenberg develops a new non-classical mechanics that becomes one of the first full formulations of quantum mechanics.

timeline_description: Major theoretical and experimental milestones in the development of quantum mechanics and quantum theory.
title: Schrodinger develops wave mechanics
description: Erwin Schrodinger publishes wave mechanics, an equivalent formulation of quantum theory.

timeline_description: Major theoretical and experimental milestones in the development of quantum mechanics and quantum theory.
title: Born introduces the probability interpretation
description: Max Born interprets the wave function statistically, a foundational move in quantum theory.

timeline_description: Major theoretical and experimental milestones in the development of quantum mechanics and quantum theory.
title: Heisenberg states the uncertainty principle
description: The uncertainty principle places fundamental limits on simultaneous knowledge of complementary quantities.

timeline_description: Major theoretical and experimental milestones in the development of quantum mechanics and quantum theory.
title: Solvay Conference frames the Copenhagen debates
description: Leading physicists debate the meaning of the new quantum theory at the fifth Solvay Conference.

timeline_description: Major theoretical and experimental milestones in the development of quantum mechanics and quantum theory.
title: Dirac formulates relativistic quantum theory of the electron
description: Paul Dirac's equation unifies quantum mechanics with special relativity for the electron and implies antimatter.

timeline_description: Major theoretical and experimental milestones in the development of quantum mechanics and quantum theory.
title: Antimatter is experimentally detected
description: Carl Anderson discovers the positron, confirming a major prediction of relativistic quantum theory.

timeline_description: Major theoretical and experimental milestones in the development of quantum mechanics and quantum theory.
title: EPR paradox challenges completeness
description: Einstein, Podolsky, and Rosen publish a thought experiment criticizing the completeness of quantum mechanics.

timeline_description: Major theoretical and experimental milestones in the development of quantum mechanics and quantum theory.
title: Schrodinger names entanglement
description: Erwin Schrodinger introduces the term entanglement while probing the conceptual consequences of quantum theory.

timeline_description: Major theoretical and experimental milestones in the development of quantum mechanics and quantum theory.
title: Bell derives Bell's theorem
description: John Bell shows how hidden-variable theories can be tested against quantum mechanics.

timeline_description: Major theoretical and experimental milestones in the development of quantum mechanics and quantum theory.
title: Aspect experiments support quantum nonlocal correlations
description: Alain Aspect's experiments provide strong evidence against local hidden-variable explanations.

timeline_description: Major theoretical and experimental milestones in the development of quantum mechanics and quantum theory.
title: Shor reveals a quantum computing breakthrough
description: Peter Shor shows that a quantum computer could efficiently factor large integers, energizing quantum information science.

timeline_description: Major theoretical and experimental milestones in the development of quantum mechanics and quantum theory.
title: Quantum supremacy claim is announced
description: Google researchers report a quantum processor performing a specialized task faster than a classical supercomputer benchmark.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of the Telephone
timeline_description: Major technical and commercial milestones in the invention, spread, and transformation of the telephone.
title: Robert Hooke experiments with a string telephone
description: Early mechanical sound transmission experiments foreshadow later telephone concepts.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of the Telephone
timeline_description: Major technical and commercial milestones in the invention, spread, and transformation of the telephone.
title: Charles Bourseul proposes electrical speech transmission
description: Bourseul describes a theoretical method for transmitting speech electrically.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of the Telephone
timeline_description: Major technical and commercial milestones in the invention, spread, and transformation of the telephone.
title: Johann Philipp Reis demonstrates a telephone device
description: Reis builds apparatus capable of transmitting some sounds electrically, though not reliable speech.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of the Telephone
timeline_description: Major technical and commercial milestones in the invention, spread, and transformation of the telephone.
title: Bell receives a key telephone patent
description: Alexander Graham Bell receives U.S. patent protection for a device to transmit speech over wires.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of the Telephone
timeline_description: Major technical and commercial milestones in the invention, spread, and transformation of the telephone.
title: First intelligible telephone call
description: Bell and Thomas Watson complete the first famous intelligible telephone call.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of the Telephone
timeline_description: Major technical and commercial milestones in the invention, spread, and transformation of the telephone.
title: Bell Telephone Company is formed
description: The Bell Telephone Company is established to commercialize the new technology.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of the Telephone
timeline_description: Major technical and commercial milestones in the invention, spread, and transformation of the telephone.
title: First commercial telephone exchange opens
description: A commercial exchange begins operating in New Haven, an important step toward networked telephony.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of the Telephone
timeline_description: Major technical and commercial milestones in the invention, spread, and transformation of the telephone.
title: The first telephone directory appears
description: The spread of subscribers creates demand for directories and standardized network management.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of the Telephone
timeline_description: Major technical and commercial milestones in the invention, spread, and transformation of the telephone.
title: Automatic exchange concepts are pursued
description: Growing network complexity pushes inventors toward switching systems that reduce dependence on operators.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of the Telephone
timeline_description: Major technical and commercial milestones in the invention, spread, and transformation of the telephone.
title: The modern wall telephone enters common use
description: A practical wall-mounted instrument by Charles Williams, Jr., becomes widely used.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of the Telephone
timeline_description: Major technical and commercial milestones in the invention, spread, and transformation of the telephone.
title: Strowger patents the automatic telephone exchange
description: Almon Strowger's switching system transforms telephone exchanges and network scaling.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of the Telephone
timeline_description: Major technical and commercial milestones in the invention, spread, and transformation of the telephone.
title: First transcontinental call is completed
description: A ceremonial call links New York and San Francisco, showing national long-distance reach.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of the Telephone
timeline_description: Major technical and commercial milestones in the invention, spread, and transformation of the telephone.
title: Commercial transatlantic telephone service begins
description: Telephone service across the Atlantic becomes commercially available.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of the Telephone
timeline_description: Major technical and commercial milestones in the invention, spread, and transformation of the telephone.
title: The transistor is invented
description: The transistor later enables smaller, more reliable, and more powerful telephone equipment.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of the Telephone
timeline_description: Major technical and commercial milestones in the invention, spread, and transformation of the telephone.
title: Telstar expands live transatlantic communications
description: Communications satellites strengthen long-distance telephone and television transmission.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of the Telephone
timeline_description: Major technical and commercial milestones in the invention, spread, and transformation of the telephone.
title: Touch-tone service is introduced
description: Push-button dialing begins to replace rotary dialing in commercial service.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of the Telephone
timeline_description: Major technical and commercial milestones in the invention, spread, and transformation of the telephone.
title: First handheld mobile phone call is made
description: Motorola engineer Martin Cooper places a landmark call from a handheld cellular prototype.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of the Telephone
timeline_description: Major technical and commercial milestones in the invention, spread, and transformation of the telephone.
title: The first commercial cellular phone is released
description: The Motorola DynaTAC 8000X symbolizes the start of the handheld cellular era.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of the Telephone
timeline_description: Major technical and commercial milestones in the invention, spread, and transformation of the telephone.
title: The first text message is sent
description: SMS messaging becomes an important new mode of mobile communication.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of the Telephone
timeline_description: Major technical and commercial milestones in the invention, spread, and transformation of the telephone.
title: The smartphone era accelerates
description: The launch of the iPhone helps popularize a powerful new model of internet-connected mobile telephony.

timeline_description: Major legal, medical, activist, and political milestones in the modern struggle for LGBTQ+ rights.
title: France decriminalizes same-sex relations
description: Revolutionary France removes sodomy from the criminal code, an early modern decriminalization milestone.

timeline_description: Major legal, medical, activist, and political milestones in the modern struggle for LGBTQ+ rights.
title: The term homosexual is coined
description: New medical and legal vocabulary begins to shape modern discussion of sexual identity.

timeline_description: Major legal, medical, activist, and political milestones in the modern struggle for LGBTQ+ rights.
title: Scientific-Humanitarian Committee is founded
description: Magnus Hirschfeld and allies create an early organization campaigning against anti-gay laws.

timeline_description: Major legal, medical, activist, and political milestones in the modern struggle for LGBTQ+ rights.
title: Society for Human Rights is founded in Chicago
description: One of the earliest known U.S. gay rights organizations is established.

timeline_description: Major legal, medical, activist, and political milestones in the modern struggle for LGBTQ+ rights.
title: COC is founded in the Netherlands
description: The COC becomes the oldest existing LGBTQ rights organization.

timeline_description: Major legal, medical, activist, and political milestones in the modern struggle for LGBTQ+ rights.
title: Mattachine Society is founded
description: The Mattachine Society becomes a pioneering gay rights organization in the United States.

timeline_description: Major legal, medical, activist, and political milestones in the modern struggle for LGBTQ+ rights.
title: Daughters of Bilitis is founded
description: The first durable lesbian rights organization in the United States is founded.

timeline_description: Major legal, medical, activist, and political milestones in the modern struggle for LGBTQ+ rights.
title: England and Wales partially decriminalize male homosexual acts
description: A major legal reform removes criminal penalties for some private consensual acts.

timeline_description: Major legal, medical, activist, and political milestones in the modern struggle for LGBTQ+ rights.
title: Stonewall uprising begins
description: Resistance to a police raid at the Stonewall Inn becomes a catalyst for modern LGBTQ activism.

timeline_description: Major legal, medical, activist, and political milestones in the modern struggle for LGBTQ+ rights.
title: First Pride marches are held
description: Anniversary demonstrations in several U.S. cities inaugurate the annual Pride tradition.

timeline_description: Major legal, medical, activist, and political milestones in the modern struggle for LGBTQ+ rights.
title: American Psychiatric Association removes homosexuality from the DSM
description: A landmark medical decision weakens the pathologization of gay identity.

timeline_description: Major legal, medical, activist, and political milestones in the modern struggle for LGBTQ+ rights.
title: Rainbow flag is first flown
description: Gilbert Baker's rainbow flag becomes an enduring symbol of LGBTQ identity and activism.

timeline_description: Major legal, medical, activist, and political milestones in the modern struggle for LGBTQ+ rights.
title: Denmark recognizes registered partnerships
description: Denmark becomes the first country to create a national legal framework for same-sex couples.

timeline_description: Major legal, medical, activist, and political milestones in the modern struggle for LGBTQ+ rights.
title: WHO removes homosexuality from its international disease classification
description: The World Health Organization's action becomes a global medical milestone.

timeline_description: Major legal, medical, activist, and political milestones in the modern struggle for LGBTQ+ rights.
title: Don't Ask, Don't Tell takes effect
description: The United States adopts a compromise policy that still bars open military service by gay personnel.

timeline_description: Major legal, medical, activist, and political milestones in the modern struggle for LGBTQ+ rights.
title: The Netherlands legalizes same-sex marriage
description: The Netherlands becomes the first country to legalize same-sex marriage.

timeline_description: Major legal, medical, activist, and political milestones in the modern struggle for LGBTQ+ rights.
title: Lawrence v. Texas strikes down U.S. sodomy laws
description: The U.S. Supreme Court invalidates remaining state sodomy laws.

timeline_description: Major legal, medical, activist, and political milestones in the modern struggle for LGBTQ+ rights.
title: The United States ends Don't Ask, Don't Tell
description: Openly gay, lesbian, and bisexual people can serve in the U.S. military.

timeline_description: Major legal, medical, activist, and political milestones in the modern struggle for LGBTQ+ rights.
title: Obergefell v. Hodges legalizes same-sex marriage nationwide in the U.S.
description: The U.S. Supreme Court recognizes a constitutional right to same-sex marriage.

timeline_description: Major legal, medical, activist, and political milestones in the modern struggle for LGBTQ+ rights.
title: Stonewall National Monument Visitor Center opens
description: A major new public history institution dedicated to LGBTQ history opens at the Stonewall site.

timeline_title: Timeline of the Great Migration in the United States
timeline_description: Major demographic, cultural, political, and social milestones in the Great Migration of African Americans in the United States.
title: Most Black Americans still live in the South
description: On the eve of the migration, the overwhelming majority of African Americans live in the Southern states.

timeline_title: Timeline of the Great Migration in the United States
timeline_description: Major demographic, cultural, political, and social milestones in the Great Migration of African Americans in the United States.
title: The first wave of the Great Migration begins
description: Large numbers of African Americans begin leaving the South for Northern cities.

timeline_title: Timeline of the Great Migration in the United States
timeline_description: Major demographic, cultural, political, and social milestones in the Great Migration of African Americans in the United States.
title: The East St. Louis race massacre shocks migrants and reformers
description: Racial violence in an industrial city highlights both opportunity and danger in the North.

timeline_title: Timeline of the Great Migration in the United States
timeline_description: Major demographic, cultural, political, and social milestones in the Great Migration of African Americans in the United States.
title: Red Summer reveals nationwide racial tensions
description: Racial violence erupts across U.S. cities as migration transforms urban demographics.

timeline_title: Timeline of the Great Migration in the United States
timeline_description: Major demographic, cultural, political, and social milestones in the Great Migration of African Americans in the United States.
title: Northern Black urban communities expand rapidly
description: Census-era demographic change confirms the scale of migration into cities such as Chicago, Detroit, and New York.

timeline_title: Timeline of the Great Migration in the United States
timeline_description: Major demographic, cultural, political, and social milestones in the Great Migration of African Americans in the United States.
title: Tulsa massacre underscores persistent insecurity
description: The destruction of Greenwood shows that migration did not end racial terror in the United States.

timeline_title: Timeline of the Great Migration in the United States
timeline_description: Major demographic, cultural, political, and social milestones in the Great Migration of African Americans in the United States.
title: Marcus Garvey is imprisoned on mail fraud charges
description: The downfall of Garvey affects one of the most prominent Black mass movements of the migration era.

timeline_title: Timeline of the Great Migration in the United States
timeline_description: Major demographic, cultural, political, and social milestones in the Great Migration of African Americans in the United States.
title: Harlem Renaissance reaches national prominence
description: The migration helps turn Harlem into the symbolic capital of modern Black cultural life.

timeline_title: Timeline of the Great Migration in the United States
timeline_description: Major demographic, cultural, political, and social milestones in the Great Migration of African Americans in the United States.
title: The Great Depression slows migration
description: Economic collapse disrupts job opportunities and temporarily reduces migratory momentum.

timeline_title: Timeline of the Great Migration in the United States
timeline_description: Major demographic, cultural, political, and social milestones in the Great Migration of African Americans in the United States.
title: A second wave begins to build
description: New wartime and industrial opportunities set the stage for a renewed and larger migratory movement.

timeline_title: Timeline of the Great Migration in the United States
timeline_description: Major demographic, cultural, political, and social milestones in the Great Migration of African Americans in the United States.
title: Defense mobilization opens industrial jobs
description: War production expands urban employment and accelerates Black migration to the North and West.

timeline_title: Timeline of the Great Migration in the United States
timeline_description: Major demographic, cultural, political, and social milestones in the Great Migration of African Americans in the United States.
title: The Second Great Migration gathers speed
description: The wartime labor market drives large-scale movement to industrial and defense centers.

timeline_title: Timeline of the Great Migration in the United States
timeline_description: Major demographic, cultural, political, and social milestones in the Great Migration of African Americans in the United States.
title: Detroit race riot exposes urban conflict
description: Competition over work and housing erupts into deadly violence in one of the migration's major destination cities.

timeline_title: Timeline of the Great Migration in the United States
timeline_description: Major demographic, cultural, political, and social milestones in the Great Migration of African Americans in the United States.
title: Postwar migration continues
description: Migration does not stop with the war; it continues into a new phase shaped by housing, education, and civil rights struggles.

timeline_title: Timeline of the Great Migration in the United States
timeline_description: Major demographic, cultural, political, and social milestones in the Great Migration of African Americans in the United States.
title: Brown v. Board of Education energizes activists
description: The civil rights movement gains a legal victory that resonates strongly in Black communities shaped by migration.

timeline_title: Timeline of the Great Migration in the United States
timeline_description: Major demographic, cultural, political, and social milestones in the Great Migration of African Americans in the United States.
title: Civil Rights Act is enacted
description: Federal civil rights legislation transforms the legal landscape for migrants and their descendants.

timeline_title: Timeline of the Great Migration in the United States
timeline_description: Major demographic, cultural, political, and social milestones in the Great Migration of African Americans in the United States.
title: Voting Rights Act is enacted
description: The act strengthens Black political power in both the South and the cities reshaped by migration.

timeline_title: Timeline of the Great Migration in the United States
timeline_description: Major demographic, cultural, political, and social milestones in the Great Migration of African Americans in the United States.
title: Urban uprisings mark a new era of crisis
description: Rebellions in cities such as Detroit and Newark reflect deep frustration over inequality in migration-era neighborhoods.

timeline_title: Timeline of the Great Migration in the United States
timeline_description: Major demographic, cultural, political, and social milestones in the Great Migration of African Americans in the United States.
title: The Great Migration is commonly dated as ending
description: By 1970 nearly half of Black Americans live outside the South, marking the end point often used for the migration.

timeline_title: Timeline of the Great Migration in the United States
timeline_description: Major demographic, cultural, political, and social milestones in the Great Migration of African Americans in the United States.
title: The migration's cultural and political legacy is entrenched
description: By the mid-1970s the migration has reshaped U.S. cities, music, literature, labor, and electoral politics.

timeline_description: Major literary milestones in the emergence, spread, and transformation of detective fiction.
title: Poe publishes The Murders in the Rue Morgue
description: Edgar Allan Poe's story is widely regarded as the first detective story.

timeline_description: Major literary milestones in the emergence, spread, and transformation of detective fiction.
title: The Purloined Letter appears
description: Poe further develops C. Auguste Dupin and the analytic detective model.

timeline_description: Major literary milestones in the emergence, spread, and transformation of detective fiction.
title: Wilkie Collins publishes The Moonstone
description: The Moonstone becomes one of the first major English detective novels.

timeline_description: Major literary milestones in the emergence, spread, and transformation of detective fiction.
title: Sherlock Holmes debuts in A Study in Scarlet
description: Arthur Conan Doyle introduces the detective who becomes the genre's defining figure.

timeline_description: Major literary milestones in the emergence, spread, and transformation of detective fiction.
title: The Strand Magazine begins publishing Holmes stories
description: Regular periodical publication helps make Sherlock Holmes a mass cultural phenomenon.

timeline_description: Major literary milestones in the emergence, spread, and transformation of detective fiction.
title: The Hound of the Baskervilles is serialized
description: One of the most famous Holmes stories reinforces the detective story's international popularity.

timeline_description: Major literary milestones in the emergence, spread, and transformation of detective fiction.
title: Chesterton's Father Brown stories begin
description: G. K. Chesterton adds a new deductive detective rooted in moral and psychological insight.

timeline_description: Major literary milestones in the emergence, spread, and transformation of detective fiction.
title: Agatha Christie publishes The Mysterious Affair at Styles
description: Christie introduces Hercule Poirot and begins one of the genre's most influential careers.

timeline_description: Major literary milestones in the emergence, spread, and transformation of detective fiction.
title: The Detection Club is founded
description: Leading British mystery writers form a club that symbolizes the classic puzzle tradition.

timeline_description: Major literary milestones in the emergence, spread, and transformation of detective fiction.
title: Dashiell Hammett publishes Red Harvest
description: American hard-boiled fiction gains force with Hammett's violence, corruption, and urban realism.

timeline_description: Major literary milestones in the emergence, spread, and transformation of detective fiction.
title: Hammett publishes The Maltese Falcon
description: The private-eye novel reaches a new level of influence and style.

timeline_description: Major literary milestones in the emergence, spread, and transformation of detective fiction.
title: Christie publishes Murder on the Orient Express
description: The puzzle detective novel attains one of its most enduring classic forms.

timeline_description: Major literary milestones in the emergence, spread, and transformation of detective fiction.
title: Chandler publishes The Big Sleep
description: Philip Marlowe becomes a defining hard-boiled detective in American fiction.

timeline_description: Major literary milestones in the emergence, spread, and transformation of detective fiction.
title: The golden age reaches a mature form
description: By the early 1940s the British clue-puzzle detective novel has reached its canonical shape.

timeline_description: Major literary milestones in the emergence, spread, and transformation of detective fiction.
title: Police procedural fiction expands
description: Postwar detective fiction increasingly focuses on teams, institutions, and realistic police work.

timeline_description: Major literary milestones in the emergence, spread, and transformation of detective fiction.
title: P. D. James launches Adam Dalgliesh
description: P. D. James helps renew British detective fiction with literary depth and psychological complexity.

timeline_description: Major literary milestones in the emergence, spread, and transformation of detective fiction.
title: Umberto Eco publishes The Name of the Rose
description: Historical detective fiction gains global prestige through a philosophically rich bestseller.

timeline_description: Major literary milestones in the emergence, spread, and transformation of detective fiction.
title: Sara Paretsky's V. I. Warshawski debuts
description: A major feminist turn arrives in detective fiction through the modern female private investigator.

timeline_description: Major literary milestones in the emergence, spread, and transformation of detective fiction.
title: The Secret History popularizes literary mystery crossover
description: Mystery and detective elements increasingly merge with literary fiction for mass audiences.

timeline_description: Major literary milestones in the emergence, spread, and transformation of detective fiction.
title: The Girl with the Dragon Tattoo internationalizes Nordic crime
description: Nordic crime fiction becomes a major global force in detective literature.

timeline_description: Major cultural, literary, artistic, and institutional milestones in the Harlem Renaissance.
title: The Silent Parade protests racial violence
description: A major New York demonstration signals the new political and cultural assertiveness that precedes the Harlem Renaissance.

timeline_description: Major cultural, literary, artistic, and institutional milestones in the Harlem Renaissance.
title: The Red Summer shapes a new Black militancy
description: Postwar racial violence helps intensify the search for new forms of Black expression and solidarity.

timeline_description: Major cultural, literary, artistic, and institutional milestones in the Harlem Renaissance.
title: Shuffle Along becomes a landmark success
description: The musical brings Black performers and creators to wider national attention and energizes Harlem nightlife.

timeline_description: Major cultural, literary, artistic, and institutional milestones in the Harlem Renaissance.
title: Claude McKay publishes Harlem Shadows
description: McKay's collection becomes a major literary milestone of the emerging movement.

timeline_description: Major cultural, literary, artistic, and institutional milestones in the Harlem Renaissance.
title: Jean Toomer publishes Cane
description: Toomer's modernist work becomes one of the signal texts of the movement.

timeline_description: Major cultural, literary, artistic, and institutional milestones in the Harlem Renaissance.
title: Civic Club dinner spotlights Black writers
description: An influential dinner in New York helps introduce emerging Black authors to publishers and patrons.

timeline_description: Major cultural, literary, artistic, and institutional milestones in the Harlem Renaissance.
title: Alain Locke publishes The New Negro
description: The anthology becomes a defining manifesto and showcase for the movement.

timeline_description: Major cultural, literary, artistic, and institutional milestones in the Harlem Renaissance.
title: Survey Graphic releases its Harlem issue
description: The special issue helps publicize Harlem as the symbolic center of a new Black cultural era.

timeline_description: Major cultural, literary, artistic, and institutional milestones in the Harlem Renaissance.
title: Opportunity awards strengthen Black literary careers
description: The magazine's literary prizes help launch important writers of the Renaissance.

timeline_description: Major cultural, literary, artistic, and institutional milestones in the Harlem Renaissance.
title: Langston Hughes publishes The Weary Blues
description: Hughes emerges as one of the signature poetic voices of the Harlem Renaissance.

timeline_description: Major cultural, literary, artistic, and institutional milestones in the Harlem Renaissance.
title: Fire!! appears
description: The short-lived magazine reflects a younger and more radical wing of the movement.

timeline_description: Major cultural, literary, artistic, and institutional milestones in the Harlem Renaissance.
title: Aaron Douglas illustrates God's Trombones
description: Douglas's visual style helps define the movement's modern Black aesthetics.

timeline_description: Major cultural, literary, artistic, and institutional milestones in the Harlem Renaissance.
title: Home to Harlem wins major readership
description: Claude McKay's novel becomes one of the movement's bestselling works.

timeline_description: Major cultural, literary, artistic, and institutional milestones in the Harlem Renaissance.
title: Wallace Thurman publishes The Blacker the Berry
description: The novel confronts colorism and modern Black identity with unusual frankness.

timeline_description: Major cultural, literary, artistic, and institutional milestones in the Harlem Renaissance.
title: The stock market crash disrupts patronage
description: The Great Depression weakens many of the institutions and networks sustaining the movement.

timeline_description: Major cultural, literary, artistic, and institutional milestones in the Harlem Renaissance.
title: Countee Cullen publishes The Black Christ and Other Poems
description: Cullen remains one of the movement's central poetic figures as the era shifts.

timeline_description: Major cultural, literary, artistic, and institutional milestones in the Harlem Renaissance.
title: Scottsboro case becomes a cultural rallying point
description: Writers and artists of the movement engage publicly with injustice and political struggle.

timeline_description: Major cultural, literary, artistic, and institutional milestones in the Harlem Renaissance.
title: Zora Neale Hurston publishes Jonah's Gourd Vine
description: Hurston's fiction extends the movement's literary achievement into the 1930s.

timeline_description: Major cultural, literary, artistic, and institutional milestones in the Harlem Renaissance.
title: Harlem riot marks a turning point
description: The riot is often treated as a symbolic end point for the classic phase of the Harlem Renaissance.

timeline_description: Major cultural, literary, artistic, and institutional milestones in the Harlem Renaissance.
title: Their Eyes Were Watching God appears after the movement's peak
description: Hurston's novel stands as a lasting achievement that grew from the artistic world shaped by the Renaissance.
